decisions.rationale: CrisisBar is an established brownfield prototype shell (Index, Onboarding, Reassessment, ResetPassword) with no IR entity on reset_pw and no named ui_ref in AUTH-02 scope (ui_refs: AAL only). Review ui_evidence passes AAL; criteria_audit has no fail/partial on crisis chrome. CrisisBar IR parity and decomposition belong to CRISIS-01-crisis-support-reusable, not AUTH-02 reset_pw page migration.

Full Button_primary_ and Input_default_ token mapping (bgcolor, border_roundness, padding) is scoped to DS-01-design-tokens-and-bubble-styles in MOD-DRSAM-DESIGN-SYSTEM — same pattern noted for AUTH-01. Review criteria_audit and ui_evidence pass: data-style-ref attributes are present and shadcn variants supply visual styling. Thin bubbleStyles bridge does not violate AUTH-02 ACs; AUTH-02 has empty depends_on and no downstream item gates on completing the token bridge from this screen.

AUTH-02 decompose scope covers reset_pw form hierarchy, Supabase updateUser submit workflow, and data-bubble-id markers — not explicit PKCE/recovery session bootstrap. functional_audit FV-4 passes (actionable error on expired/missing session). Relying on supabase client detectSessionInUrl is acceptable for this item; explicit PASSWORD_RECOVERY handler is hardening to revisit only if integration testing reveals a race.

summary: 0 fix_now, 3 defer, 0 accept. Advance OK — review ok=true, coverage 100%, all ACs and functional checks pass. Deferred: C-1 → CRISIS-01-crisis-support-reusable (CrisisBar IR/shell); C-2 → DS-01-design-tokens-and-bubble-styles (bubbleStyles token bridge); C-3 → future auth hardening if PKCE testing surfaces recovery-session race.
